threats.title: SurplusGuildMinter getRewards() Checks Slashed Flag Against Uninitialized Memory, Enabling Permissionless Theft of All Staked CREDIT
threats.hypothesis_description: In SurplusGuildMinter.getRewards(), the `slashed` condition is evaluated against the default-initialized (zero) `userStake` return variable BEFORE the actual user stake is loaded from storage. Since `userStake.lastGaugeLoss` is 0 in the uninitialized struct, any term where `GuildToken.lastGaugeLoss(term) > 0` will cause `slashed = true` for ALL stakers, regardless of when they staked. This zeroes the user's stake record, permanently locking their CREDIT in the term surplus buffer and orphaning their GUILD gauge weight.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a demonstrable code defect where reading a return variable before it's populated from storage causes incorrect slash processing. The function is public and callable by anyone for any user, making it a permissionless griefing/theft vector.

proof_of_signal.pattern: slashed flag evaluated against default-zero userStake.lastGaugeLoss before storage load at line 228: `userStake = _stakes[user][term]`

preconditions: A lending term has experienced at least one gauge loss (lastGaugeLoss > 0)
At least one user has an active stake in SurplusGuildMinter for that term
No special roles required - function is public

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Line 220: `if (lastGaugeLoss > uint256(userStake.lastGaugeLoss))` where userStake is still default-zero memory
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Call getRewards(victim, term) for any victim on any term with historical loss
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker does not profit directly but permanently destroys victim CREDIT stakes and orphans GUILD weight; griefing attack with 100% victim loss

economic_questions: What is the total value at risk across all SurplusGuildMinter stakes on terms with historical losses?
Can the orphaned GUILD weight cause secondary effects on debt ceilings?
Is there any recovery mechanism for the locked termSurplusBuffer CREDIT?
validation_requirements: Deploy test: stake CREDIT on a term, trigger a loss on that term, then call getRewards() and verify slashed=true
Verify that the CREDIT in termSurplusBuffer is irrecoverable after slash
Confirm orphaned GUILD gauge weight persists and affects debt ceiling calculations

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Gas cost only (~0.001 ETH per victim)
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: Minimal gas per call to getRewards()
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: No direct profit; 100% loss of victim's staked CREDIT + orphaned GUILD weight
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Pure griefing; infinite ROI for disruption

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Any time after a term has experienced a loss
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Systemic: all SGM stakers on affected terms lose funds

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All SurplusGuildMinter stakers on any term that has had a gauge loss
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Sum of all CREDIT staked via SurplusGuildMinter on affected terms

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Any term with lastGaugeLoss > 0 is affected
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Trace getRewards() line by line confirming uninitialized read

threats.title: Stale canOffboard Flag Allows Immediate Re-Offboarding After Re-Onboarding, Permanently Pausing PSM Redemptions
threats.hypothesis_description: When a lending term is offboarded, `canOffboard[term]` is set to true and `nOffboardingsInProgress` is incremented. If the term is re-onboarded by governance (via addGauge) before cleanup() is called, `canOffboard[term]` persists as true. An attacker can immediately call `offboard(term)` again without any new poll or quorum, bypassing the democratic offboarding process. This double-increments `nOffboardingsInProgress` but cleanup() can only decrement it once (setting `canOffboard[term] = false`), leaving a permanent residual that keeps PSM redemptions paused.
threats.investigation_rationale: The canOffboard mapping is never reset when a term is re-onboarded, creating a state inconsistency. Combined with the nOffboardingsInProgress counter tracking PSM pause state, this creates a permanent DoS on PSM redemptions.

proof_of_signal.pattern: offboard() only checks canOffboard[term] which persists from previous offboarding cycle; no check that the term is currently in an active offboarding round

preconditions: A term was previously offboarded (canOffboard = true)
Governance re-onboarded the term without cleanup being called first
Attacker can call offboard() - no special roles needed

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: nOffboardingsInProgress incremented in offboard() but only decremented in cleanup() which sets canOffboard=false, preventing further decrements
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: offboard(term) called with stale canOffboard[term]=true after re-onboarding
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Permanent PSM redemption pause locks all CREDIT holders from redeeming for peg tokens; attacker profits by shorting CREDIT or holding collateral positions

economic_questions: What is the total value locked in PSM that becomes irredeemable?
Can governance recover from permanent nOffboardingsInProgress elevation?
Is there a path to trigger cleanup twice to fix the counter?
validation_requirements: Simulate: offboard term -> re-onboard -> offboard again -> verify nOffboardingsInProgress is 2
Call cleanup -> verify nOffboardingsInProgress is 1 and canOffboard is false
Verify no path to decrement nOffboardingsInProgress further

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: GUILD tokens needed for initial offboard quorum + gas
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Indirect: entire PSM peg token balance becomes locked
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Dependent on attacker's ability to profit from PSM disruption

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately after governance re-onboards a previously offboarded term
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Systemic: all CREDIT holders unable to redeem via PSM

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All CREDIT holders wanting to redeem via PSM
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Unable to redeem CREDIT for peg tokens

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check if any terms have been re-onboarded without cleanup in deployment

threats.title: Debt Ceiling Check in GuildToken._decrementGaugeWeight Locks GUILD Voters When Term Is Fully Utilized
threats.hypothesis_description: GuildToken overrides _decrementGaugeWeight to check that `issuance <= debtCeilingAfterDecrement`. When a lending term is fully utilizing its debt ceiling (issuance near/at ceiling), ALL voters for that term are locked: they cannot decrement gauge weight, transfer tokens, or burn tokens. The _decrementWeightUntilFree function (called on transfer/burn) reverts when attempting to free gauge weights from fully-utilized terms. A malicious borrower could deliberately keep a term at max utilization to freeze all GUILD voters on that term.
threats.investigation_rationale: This creates a griefing vector where borrowers can lock GUILD token holders by maintaining maximum utilization. Additionally, applyGaugeLoss (which calls _decrementGaugeWeight) would also revert, preventing the slashing of voters who should be punished for bad debt.

proof_of_signal.pattern: require(issuance <= debtCeilingAfterDecrement) blocks weight removal when term is at capacity; also blocks applyGaugeLoss

preconditions: A lending term is borrowing at or near its debt ceiling
GUILD holders have weight allocated to this term
Borrowers maintain utilization to prevent weight removal

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: debtCeiling(-int256(weight)) would reduce the ceiling below current issuance, causing revert
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Borrower keeps term at max utilization; all GUILD voters on that term are frozen
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Borrower avoids being liquidated or having term offboarded; voters lose liquidity on GUILD tokens

economic_questions: What is the cost for a borrower to maintain max utilization vs the value locked for GUILD voters?
Can applyGaugeLoss be blocked by maintaining utilization, preventing slashing after bad debt?
Is there a recovery path for voters beyond waiting for borrowers to repay?
validation_requirements: Test: borrow to max capacity, then try to decrementGauge - verify revert
Test: trigger gauge loss while term is at max capacity, then try applyGaugeLoss - verify revert
Quantify the GUILD tokens that would be locked per unit of borrowed CREDIT

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Griefing: locks all GUILD voters' tokens on the term
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Indirect benefit: attacker avoids slashing, voters lose token mobility

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Sufficient collateral to borrow up to debt ceiling
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When the borrower wants to prevent offboarding or avoid slashing
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Locks GUILD token liquidity; prevents governance from functioning for affected term

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All GUILD voters on the affected term
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Complete inability to transfer, burn, or re-allocate GUILD tokens allocated to that term
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Total GUILD weight allocated to the affected term

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check all active terms for utilization ratios
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Model debtCeiling reduction from weight removal vs issuance

threats.title: GuildVetoGovernor Deterministic ProposalId Prevents Repeated Veto Attempts on Same Timelock Action
threats.hypothesis_description: GuildVetoGovernor.createVeto() generates a deterministic proposalId from the timelockId (via the OZ Governor's propose mechanism hashing targets/values/calldatas/description). Since all these parameters are deterministic for a given timelockId, the same proposalId is generated each time. If a veto proposal fails to reach quorum (state = Defeated), OZ Governor prevents creating a new proposal with the same proposalId. An attacker can create a veto proposal early when few votes are available, ensuring it fails, permanently preventing legitimate veto attempts on that timelock action.
threats.investigation_rationale: The veto mechanism is critical for the honest minority security assumption. If vetos can be burned by early creation, the entire governance security model is undermined. Any malicious timelock action could be protected from veto.

proof_of_signal.pattern: createVeto builds deterministic proposal parameters from timelockId; OZ Governor prevents duplicate proposalIds; once Defeated, no retry possible

preconditions: A timelock action is pending (timelockExecutionTime > 1)
Attacker creates veto before legitimate voters are aware/organized
Veto fails to reach quorum before timelock action becomes executable

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: proposalId = hashProposal(targets, values, calldatas, descriptionHash) is deterministic for a given timelockId
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Create veto immediately when action is queued; insufficient votes means it fails; no retry possible
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker protects a malicious governance action from veto, enabling extraction of value through the queued action

economic_questions: What governance actions could bypass veto protection this way?
How much value could be extracted through an unvetoed malicious governance action?
Is there a workaround (e.g., requeue the same action with different salt)?
validation_requirements: Create veto proposal -> let it fail -> attempt to create same veto again -> verify revert
Check if OZ Governor state Defeated prevents re-proposing with same hash
Assess all critical timelock operations that could be targeted

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Any token holder can create veto (no minimum threshold)
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Depends on the timelock action being protected
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Potentially massive if protecting a malicious governance action
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Single transaction to burn veto capability; permanent effect

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Token for voting weight not needed for creation, only for voting
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Immediately when a malicious timelock action is queued
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Undermines the entire honest minority governance assumption

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All token holders who rely on veto as security mechanism
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Entire protocol TVL if unvetoed action is sufficiently malicious

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Review OZ Governor.propose for duplicate proposalId handling
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Hash parameters for same timelockId and verify proposalId determinism

threats.title: ProfitManager.notifyPnL Reverts on Loss Exceeding Credit Total Supply, Permanently Bricking Auction Settlement
threats.hypothesis_description: In ProfitManager.notifyPnL(), when processing a loss that exceeds the surplus buffer, the creditMultiplier is updated via: `newCreditMultiplier = (creditMultiplier * (creditTotalSupply - loss)) / creditTotalSupply`. If `loss > creditTotalSupply`, this underflows and reverts. After prior creditMultiplier reductions, loan principal is amplified: `principal = borrowAmount * borrowCreditMultiplier / creditMultiplier`. If creditMultiplier has dropped significantly, the amplified principal can exceed total CREDIT supply, making the loss unprocessable. The auction calling onBid→notifyPnL will permanently revert, locking collateral and breaking the liquidation system.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a critical arithmetic underflow in the loss processing path. While it requires specific market conditions (cascading losses), the scenario is realistic in severe market downturns and would permanently brick the affected loan's liquidation.

proof_of_signal.pattern: `(creditMultiplier * (creditTotalSupply - loss)) / creditTotalSupply` underflows when loss > creditTotalSupply after surplus buffer depletion

preconditions: creditMultiplier has been reduced from prior losses (e.g., to 0.01e18)
A loan opened when creditMultiplier was higher defaults
Amplified principal = borrowAmount * (oldMultiplier / currentMultiplier) > creditTotalSupply - surplusBuffer

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: principal = borrowAmount * borrowCreditMultiplier / creditMultiplier; if borrowCreditMultiplier was 1e18 and creditMultiplier is 0.01e18, principal = 100x borrowAmount
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Cascading losses drive creditMultiplier down; old loans default with amplified principal
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: System becomes permanently stuck; collateral locked in LendingTerm

economic_questions: What creditMultiplier reduction threshold makes this exploitable?
How much total CREDIT supply is needed to prevent this for given loan sizes?
Can an attacker deliberately drive creditMultiplier down to trigger this on their own loan?
validation_requirements: Model: set creditMultiplier to 0.1e18, open loan with original multiplier 1e18, default, verify revert
Calculate minimum creditMultiplier threshold for various loan sizes
Assess cascade risk across multiple terms

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Multiple collateral positions to drive cascading losses
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Systemic disruption; locked collateral value depends on positions
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Difficult to profit directly; primarily a DoS vector

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Collateral for multiple loans across multiple terms
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: During market stress when creditMultiplier is already declining
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Can permanently brick the liquidation system

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All CREDIT holders (loss cannot be processed), collateral bidders (can't bid)
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Collateral in affected loans + systemic CREDIT value

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Compute exact thresholds for underflow based on realistic creditMultiplier ranges
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Fuzzing notifyPnL with various loss/supply/multiplier combinations

threats.title: GUILD Profit Permanently Stuck When Gauge Weight Is Zero During Profit Notification
threats.hypothesis_description: In ProfitManager.notifyPnL(), when profit is distributed and the guild split is computed, the code checks `if (_gaugeWeight != 0)` before updating `gaugeProfitIndex`. If the gauge weight is 0 (e.g., during offboarding when all voters have removed weight), the CREDIT tokens allocated as `amountForGuild` are subtracted from the total but never made claimable. They remain on the ProfitManager contract with no mechanism to recover them. This is a permanent loss of protocol revenue.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a natural occurrence during the offboarding lifecycle: a term is deprecated, voters remove weight (weight→0), but active loans may still be repaying interest. The interest split directed to guild stakers becomes permanently stuck.

proof_of_signal.pattern: amountForGuild computed and subtracted from total, but gaugeProfitIndex only updated if _gaugeWeight != 0; tokens stuck when weight is 0

preconditions: A lending term has active loans generating interest
The term's gauge weight drops to 0 (e.g., after offboarding and voter exit)
Loan repayment or auction triggers notifyPnL with positive amount

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: amountForGuild = amount * guildSplit / 1e9; if gaugeWeight == 0, this is deducted but never indexed
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: CREDIT tokens permanently locked = guildSplit * total_interest_paid_when_weight_is_zero

economic_questions: How much interest is likely to accrue during offboarding periods when gauge weight is 0?
Could amountForGuild be redirected to surplus buffer or credit holders instead?
Is there a governance recovery mechanism via emergencyAction?
validation_requirements: Deprecate gauge, remove all weight, repay a loan with interest, verify CREDIT balance stuck on ProfitManager
Quantify stuck amount vs total protocol revenue

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: N/A - this is a loss, not an exploit
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Ongoing whenever interest is paid to 0-weight gauge

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: During any offboarding with outstanding loans

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Proportional share of lost guild split
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: guildSplit percentage of all interest from 0-weight gauges

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: guildSplit from profitSharingConfig and typical interest flows
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Track CREDIT balance of ProfitManager before/after notifyPnL with 0-weight gauge

threats.title: ProfitSharingConfig Precision Loss from uint32 Truncation Causes Systematic Profit Misdistribution
threats.hypothesis_description: ProfitManager.setProfitSharingConfig() validates that splits sum to 1e18 at 18-decimal precision, then stores them as uint32 values after dividing by 1e9. This truncation loses up to (1e9 - 1) per split. When splits are read back via getProfitSharingConfig(), they're multiplied by 1e9, and creditSplit is computed as the residual (1e18 - surplusBufferSplit - guildSplit - otherSplit). The truncation means the actual distribution differs from configured intent, with the residual creditSplit absorbing the error. Over many distributions, this systematic error compounds.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a demonstrable precision error in the storage packing optimization. While individual impact per distribution is small, it represents incorrect code that accumulates over the protocol's lifetime.

proof_of_signal.pattern: uint32(surplusBufferSplit / 1e9) truncates; configured splits summing to 1e18 may not reconstruct accurately after storage round-trip

preconditions: setProfitSharingConfig is called with values not evenly divisible by 1e9
Profit is distributed through notifyPnL

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Governor configures splits with values not aligned to 1e9 boundary
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: creditSplit absorbs truncation error; up to ~3e9 total rounding error across 3 stored splits per distribution

economic_questions: What is the maximum per-distribution misdistribution in absolute CREDIT terms?
Does the error systematically favor credit holders (residual absorbs positive truncation)?
Over protocol lifetime, what is the cumulative impact?
validation_requirements: Set splits with values not divisible by 1e9, read back, compute difference
Calculate amountForGuild and amountForCredit with truncated vs intended splits

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to ~3 gwei per split per distribution cycle (requires validation)
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Negligible per transaction, cumulative over time

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Minimal per-event, cumulative over protocol lifetime

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: GUILD voters or surplus buffer (whichever loses from truncation)

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check actual configured split values vs 1e9 divisibility
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Compute exact truncation error for realistic split configurations

threats.title: ERC20RebaseDistributor distribute() Restarts Interpolation Period, Enabling Perpetual Reward Delay Griefing
threats.hypothesis_description: Each call to distribute() resets the interpolation period for __rebasingSharePrice and __unmintedRebaseRewards to a new 30-day window from the current timestamp. This means that if distributions happen frequently (e.g., multiple loan repayments or an attacker calling distribute with small amounts), previously pending rewards that were partway through interpolation are effectively pushed further into the future. An attacker could burn small amounts of CREDIT to call distribute(1) repeatedly, perpetually delaying reward materialization for all rebasing CREDIT holders.
threats.investigation_rationale: The interpolation mechanism is designed for smooth reward distribution, but the restart behavior creates a griefing vector. The attacker burns tokens (cost) to delay all other users' rewards (impact), creating an asymmetric griefing opportunity.

proof_of_signal.pattern: distribute() sets lastTimestamp=block.timestamp and targetTimestamp=block.timestamp+DISTRIBUTION_PERIOD for BOTH share price and unminted rewards, resetting ALL interpolation progress

preconditions: There are pending unminted rebase rewards in interpolation
Attacker holds some CREDIT tokens to burn via distribute()
Rebasing supply > 0

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: newTargetSharePrice computed using __rebasingSharePrice.targetValue (includes all undistributed amounts) but restarts interpolation from current time
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: distribute(1) repeatedly to restart 30-day interpolation window
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker loses 1 CREDIT per call; delays rewards for ALL rebasing holders worth potentially millions

economic_questions: What is the cost per delay iteration vs total delayed rewards?
How much can rewards be practically delayed given gas costs and CREDIT cost?
Does the protocol naturally have frequent distributions that already cause this?
validation_requirements: Simulate: distribute large amount, wait 15 days (50%), then distribute(1), verify reward delay
Calculate effective APY reduction for rebasing holders under attack
Model attacker cost vs victim impact ratio

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Minimal CREDIT tokens (1 wei per call + gas)
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Griefing: delays rewards proportional to total rebasing supply
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Asymmetric: 1 CREDIT cost delays millions in rewards
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Must be repeated frequently (at least once per 30 days)

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Right before large reward distributions are about to complete interpolation
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Reduces effective yield for all rebasing CREDIT holders

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Pending unminted rebase rewards delayed indefinitely

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: DISTRIBUTION_PERIOD = 30 days; current unminted rewards; total rebasing supply
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Model interpolation restart impact on APY

threats.title: ERC20Gauges._decrementWeightUntilFree Infinite Loop When User Has Zero-Weight Gauge in Set
threats.hypothesis_description: In ERC20Gauges._decrementWeightUntilFree(), the loop iterates over user gauges to free weight. The `unchecked { ++i; }` statement is inside the `if (userGaugeWeight != 0)` block. If a gauge in the user's set has 0 weight, the loop index never increments, causing an infinite loop that exhausts gas. A zero-weight gauge can be added to the user's set by calling incrementGauge(gauge, 0). This affects transfers and burns of GUILD tokens, as both call _decrementWeightUntilFree.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a code defect where the loop counter increment is conditionally executed. While self-inflicted via incrementGauge(gauge, 0), it could also be triggered through SurplusGuildMinter if mintRatio is set to 0, affecting the contract's ability to transfer/burn GUILD tokens.

proof_of_signal.pattern: `unchecked { ++i; }` is inside `if (userGaugeWeight != 0)` block; 0-weight gauge causes infinite loop

preconditions: User (or SurplusGuildMinter) calls incrementGauge(gauge, 0), adding gauge to set with 0 weight
User later tries to transfer/burn GUILD tokens requiring _decrementWeightUntilFree
The 0-weight gauge is encountered before enough weight is freed

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: for loop: `i` only increments when `userGaugeWeight != 0`; zero-weight entry causes perpetual iteration
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: incrementGauge(gauge, 0) adds gauge to user's EnumerableSet without adding weight
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: User's GUILD tokens become permanently non-transferable; griefing if SurplusGuildMinter is affected

economic_questions: Can SurplusGuildMinter be affected if mintRatio is set to 0?
Can users self-recover by calling decrementGauge(gauge, 0)?
What is the actual gas cost of the infinite loop (OOG vs block limit)?
validation_requirements: Test: incrementGauge(gauge, 0), then transfer tokens requiring weight freeing - verify OOG
Test recovery via decrementGauge(gauge, 0)
Test SurplusGuildMinter with mintRatio=0

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Limited to affected user unless SurplusGuildMinter is impacted

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: User with 0-weight gauge in set; potentially SurplusGuildMinter
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Complete inability to transfer affected GUILD tokens
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All GUILD tokens held by affected address

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check if mintRatio can be set to 0 and if incrementGauge(gauge, 0) is callable
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify loop behavior with 0-weight gauge entry

threats.title: creditMultiplier Approaching Zero Bricks Multiple System Functions via Division by Zero
threats.hypothesis_description: creditMultiplier has no floor protection and can reach extremely low values or theoretically 0 through cascading losses. If creditMultiplier approaches 0: (1) getLoanDebt divides by creditMultiplier, amplifying all debts toward infinity; (2) SimplePSM.getMintAmountOut divides by creditMultiplier, allowing infinite CREDIT minting for minimal peg tokens; (3) minBorrow() divides by creditMultiplier, potentially overflowing; (4) the multiplier update formula itself can reach 0 if loss exactly equals totalSupply. At or near 0, the entire system becomes non-functional.
threats.investigation_rationale: The creditMultiplier is a critical system parameter used as a divisor in multiple calculations. The absence of a minimum floor creates a systemic risk from cascading bad debt events.

proof_of_signal.pattern: newCreditMultiplier has no floor; if it reaches 0, division by creditMultiplier in getLoanDebt (LendingTerm.sol:243) and getMintAmountOut (SimplePSM.sol:92) causes revert

preconditions: Multiple large losses deplete surplus buffer and reduce creditMultiplier significantly
creditMultiplier approaches or reaches 0
Multiple protocol functions become non-functional

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: newCreditMultiplier = creditMultiplier * (creditTotalSupply - loss) / creditTotalSupply; if totalSupply == loss, result is 0
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Series of deliberate defaults to drive multiplier to zero
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Near-zero multiplier allows minting massive CREDIT from PSM for negligible peg tokens

economic_questions: What is the minimum realistic creditMultiplier the system could reach under severe market stress?
At what multiplier level does PSM minting become exploitable?
Could an attacker orchestrate cascading defaults to drive multiplier down?
validation_requirements: Model cascading loss scenarios to determine reachable multiplier levels
Test system behavior at multiplier values of 0.001e18, 0.0001e18, and 0
Verify getMintAmountOut outputs at near-zero multiplier

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Large collateral positions across multiple terms for cascading defaults
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Near-zero multiplier: mint unlimited CREDIT from PSM
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Potentially protocol-breaking if PSM has significant reserves

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Significant collateral capital for multiple positions
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Market downturn when collateral values are declining
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Complete protocol failure if multiplier reaches 0

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All CREDIT holders, all borrowers, all protocol participants

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Current creditMultiplier value, surplus buffer size, total issuance
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Minimum multiplier reachable under worst-case cascade

threats.title: LendingTerm.forgive() on Active Auction Permanently Blocks Auction Settlement and AuctionHouse Migration
threats.hypothesis_description: LendingTerm.forgive() does not verify whether the loan is currently in an active auction (callTime != 0). If a governor calls forgive() on a loan that has been called and has an active auction, it sets closeTime, making onBid() revert (requires closeTime == 0). The auction in AuctionHouse can never complete, permanently inflating nAuctionsInProgress. This blocks setAuctionHouse() which requires nAuctionsInProgress == 0, preventing auction house migration and leaving collateral stuck.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a state machine violation where forgive() creates an illegal state (loan closed while auction is active). While it requires GOVERNOR role, it's a code bug that could be triggered by legitimate governance actions during emergency situations.

proof_of_signal.pattern: forgive() checks borrowTime != 0 and closeTime == 0 but NOT callTime == 0; can close a loan in active auction

preconditions: GOVERNOR role (accessed via timelock in production)
A loan is in active auction (callTime != 0, closeTime == 0)
Governor calls forgive() on this loan

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: forgive sets closeTime > 0; onBid requires closeTime == 0; auction stuck forever
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: GOVERNOR calls forgive on loan in auction (possibly accidental during emergency)
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: nAuctionsInProgress permanently elevated; setAuctionHouse blocked; collateral locked

economic_questions: Can emergencyAction on AuctionHouse manually fix nAuctionsInProgress?
What is the value of collateral that could be permanently locked?
Could a compromised governor trigger this to freeze the auction system?
validation_requirements: Call loan, start auction, then forgive - verify auction.bid() reverts
